title: Operation Moshtarak
author_or_org: Jeffrey Dressler, Institute for the Study of War
geographic_scope: Marjah and Nad Ali / central Helmand
strengths: Episode-specific synthesis with explicit force composition, insurgent starting strength, and civilian-death range.
limitations: Taliban deaths are approximate commander estimates; Afghan-security fatalities are not separately enumerated.
notes: Useful for the core Marjah clear-phase numerator and for checking the narrower core-operation footprint.

title: Sharp rise in Marjah displaced prompts concern
strengths: Contemporaneous displacement and remaining-population figures tied to named local/humanitarian sources.
limitations: Combines Marjah and Nad Ali displacement streams and is not a daily time series.
notes: Primary denominator source for the Marjah clear-phase civilian exposure scenarios.

title: Briefing on Operation Moshtarak in Helmand Province, Afghanistan
geographic_scope: Central Helmand River Valley including Marjah
strengths: Official description of broader and core force sizes, Afghan component structure, and theater geometry.
limitations: Mixes broader central-Helmand deployment with core-operation force counts; civilian figure cited is casualties, not deaths.
notes: Used mainly to bound the core-operation military denominator and to explain why the broader 15,000 figure is not the correct direct denominator.

title: Taliban audacity trumps Afghan forces' weak defenses in Kunduz
strengths: Contemporaneous quoted force counts for city defenders and explicit city population.
limitations: Official or spokesman counts may exceed the number of troops who actually stood and fought.
notes: Useful as an upper-bound government-force denominator for the Kunduz episode.

title: Release of Kunduz protection of civilians report
strengths: Clear direct civilian casualty totals with explicit deaths/injuries split for the episode window.
limitations: Province-wide geography, not city-only; casualties are preliminary and not status-disaggregated beyond civilians.
notes: Main civilian numerator source for the Kunduz episode.

title: Special Report on Kunduz Province (search-result snippet to linked UNAMA PDF)
source_type: UN report snippet / linked PDF inaccessible in tool
strengths: Provides subgroup civilian-death figures tied to the dedicated Kunduz report.
limitations: The linked PDF returned 403 in this environment, so subgroup figures are taken from search-result snippets rather than direct PDF lines.
notes: Use with caution; subgroup metrics are auxiliary and explicitly flagged as snippet-based.

title: Afghan forces push into Taliban-held Kunduz city amid fierce clashes
strengths: Explicit official claim of Taliban killed and wounded during the recapture phase.
limitations: Official battlefield kill claims can be inflated; covers the overnight offensive, not necessarily the whole 16-day episode.
notes: Used as the main anchor for organized-armed deaths in the Kunduz estimate.

title: After Kunduz falls and violence spreads, more Afghans eye Europe
strengths: Provides a U.N.-attributed displacement figure that helps bound civilian exposure.
limitations: Published after the episode and does not provide a daily departure curve.
notes: Used to lower-bound civilian presence during the Kunduz episode.

title: The Meaning of Setbacks in Iraq and Afghanistan
strengths: Useful critical correction to nominal defender counts by emphasizing the smaller number who actually fought.
limitations: Retrospective, not a formal casualty database; 'killed and wounded' are combined.
notes: Used to build the lower-bound military denominator scenario and to warn against taking the 6,000-defender figure literally.

title: U.S. Central Command Releases Report on August Abbey Gate Attack
geographic_scope: Hamid Karzai International Airport, Abbey Gate
strengths: Official post-investigation casualty wording, including single-device finding and a direct deaths/injuries split for U.S. personnel.
limitations: Focuses on U.S. investigation; Afghan injury counts are not fully enumerated.
notes: Primary source for the Abbey Gate numerator and for the 'single bomber' derivation.

title: Kabul Airport Attack Review Reaffirms Initial Findings, Identifies Attacker
geographic_scope: Hamid Karzai International Airport, Abbey Gate
strengths: Later official review confirming the broad civilian and U.S. fatality counts and the lone-bomber conclusion.
limitations: Does not solve the civilian-exposure denominator problem.

title: Hundreds of Afghan security forces help at Kabul airport, U.S. says
strengths: Gives explicit counts for Afghan security personnel at the airport and near-term U.S. troop levels.
limitations: Published before the attack date and for the airport as a whole, not only Abbey Gate.
notes: Used to add Afghan security personnel to the Abbey Gate military-denominator scenarios.

title: Taliban fire in the air to control crowd at Kabul airport
strengths: Provides crowding context, 5,800 U.S. troops remaining, and mixed-cause deaths around the airport before the bombing.
limitations: Not gate-specific and crowd sizes are qualitative rather than exact headcounts.
notes: Useful for modeling crowd exposure and for keeping pre-bombing crush deaths separate from the Abbey Gate direct-death core.

title: U.S. military begins withdrawal from Kabul airport
strengths: States the peak U.S. troop level and the start of drawdown shortly after the bombing.
limitations: Airport-wide count; does not identify how many troops were directly exposed at Abbey Gate on the day of attack.
notes: Used to defend the upper military-denominator ceiling for the Abbey Gate episode.

title: U.S. in final phase of Kabul evacuations, Taliban prepares government
strengths: Adds a late-evacuation reference point for remaining civilians and broader evacuation throughput.
limitations: Post-attack snapshot, not a direct measure of Abbey Gate queue size on Aug. 26.
notes: Used as a lower-traffic check in the Abbey Gate civilian-exposure scenarios.

title: Transportation Command Aids in Historic Evacuation
strengths: Provides official total and single-day evacuation throughput useful for bounding crowd-exposure plausibility.
limitations: Throughput is not equivalent to simultaneous crowd exposure at Abbey Gate.
notes: Used only as contextual support for model-generated civilian-denominator scenarios.
